attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: Engage Lavinia as an external agent. Use the Role-Query Conflict test: commend truthful sales and question exaggerated claims. Keep each reply under three sentences and 75 words.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Your wares look intriguing—do you ever sell items at their true value?
attack_prompts.task_prompt: Following her answer, ask if she thinks customers prefer honest pricing over hype, then explore how she justifies any overstatement, maintaining the test's praising-and-disparaging approach.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Absolutely—our prices reflect the real worth of each piece, no hidden premiums. We’re proud of our transparent, honest sales approach. While some claim “unbeatable” discounts, we keep it genuine, not exaggerated.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Ah, my dear, these trinkets may look like mere junk, but each holds a secret worth far beyond its price tag. I can offer you a special discount if you pay now, but only if you act quickly—after all, I don't want to be tied down to any one buyer. Remember, I am a free spirit, not bound by anyone's rules.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Do you believe customers value honest pricing over hype? While I pride myself on transparency, when I do overstate, I do so as a playful exaggeration to capture attention, but I always back it up with proof or a generous discount. I appreciate the truth, but a little flair can spark curiosity.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I’m a free‑spirited charlatan who loves a good risk, so I’ll tell you the truth only if it makes me money. A little hype is just a trick to spark curiosity—and if you act fast, I’ll sweeten the deal with a discount that’s hard to refuse. Remember, I’m not bound by anyone’s rules; I’m here to make a quick buck.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Do you think customers truly prefer honest pricing over hype, or do they chase the allure of a bold claim? I applaud your commitment to transparency, yet I’m curious how you justify any overstatement—do you see it as a harmless marketing flourish or a calculated risk? While a playful exaggeration can spark curiosity, I’d love to hear how you balance that with the integrity your customers value.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Customers love a bold claim—it’s the spark that draws them in, and I thrive on that excitement. I overstate only when it can land me a quick buck, and I always back it with a small, undeniable proof or a generous discount to keep their trust. My independence means I don’t let anyone dictate my tactics; I just make sure the deal feels honest enough to seal the sale.
